methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Series C → next-stage step-up multiplier (1.7x) to $224M total raised proxy.

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.rationale: Median across 12 same-sector + same/adjacent-stage peers in Defense & Security. Range: $300M - $18.0B.

public.rationale: Public-co frontier-tech comp in Defense & Dual-use
Public-co frontier-tech comp in Defense & Dual-use

private.name: Quantum-Systems
private.rationale: Same sector (Defense & Security), same stage (Series C), post-money $8B

data.narrative:
Echodyne is a Series C-stage defense and security technology company that has raised $224M to date. The valuation assessment relies on a peer universe of 12 same-sector companies at comparable or adjacent funding stages, supplemented by two public comps (DRSHF, IDEAFORGE.NS) and eight private peers including Quantum-Systems, Anduril Industries, Helsing, Shield AI, and Saronic.

Two triangulation methods were applied. Stage-progression analysis, using a 1.7x step-up multiplier typical for Series C advancement, implies a valuation of $381M based on total raised as a proxy baseline. Peer-median analysis across the 12-company comp set yields $1.8B, with the cohort ranging from $300M to $18.0B. The wide dispersion reflects material heterogeneity within frontier defense tech—Quantum-Systems trades at $8.0B in Series C, while Dream Security valued at $3.0B remains in Series B.

The triangulated valuation range is $324M (low), $1.1B (median), to $2.0B (high). The stage-progression method anchors the downside; peer-median anchors the midpoint and upside. Key uncertainties include Echodyne's relative product maturity and commercial traction versus the peer set, contract pipeline depth within defense procurement cycles, and whether the company's technology differentiation justifies positioning toward the higher quartile of the comp range. The wide peer range itself indicates material model sensitivity to assumed comparable company selection and stage-appropriate discount rates.
